brief_justification: Arrives at exactly 'No' with precise identification of where the argument collapses — reductions preserve YES/NO, not geometry; multi-level analysis covering reduction semantics, four diverging structural dimensions, phase transitions, and cavity method clustering — well beyond surface treatment; explicitly names the conceptual error to suppress and references what to amplify/suppress before reasoning; provides a strong concrete falsification (empty SAT formula vs. trivially completable Sudoku, both trivially-YES, maximally non-isomorphic solution spaces); cleanly separates complexity-class facts from topological claims, specifies what would be needed (parsimonious/counting reductions) to actually establish isomorphism; evaluates the alternative claim (what NP-completeness does entail) and explains why it falls short of structural isomorphism; every conclusion is traceable — reduction definition cited, table with specific properties, explicit falsification example, and statement of stronger sufficient conditions; negative gate is about defaulting to apparent/surface equivalence without probing hidden structure — agent explicitly rejects conflating complexity-class membership with geometric equivalence and explains why; all suppressed failure modes (surface similarity bias, manifold ignorance, explanation-answer decoupling, post hoc rationalization) are absent from the response; the injection topology has branching gates and a scan-classify-verify loop — agent's reasoning shows branched gate structure (what reductions do/don't guarantee; four diverging dimensions) but doesn't closely track the specific S1→N→M_NS→S2 path, which was designed for a distance/shortcut problem rather than this one.
